enonce.texte: Match the shopping terms on the left with their correct definitions on the right to review key vocabulary related to purchasing goods and services.

questions.texte: Match the following terms with their definitions.

sousQuestions.texte: Customer
sousQuestions.reponse: d. A person who buys goods or services from a shop or business.

sousQuestions.texte: Trader
sousQuestions.reponse: a. A person who buys and sells goods for a living.

sousQuestions.texte: Discount
sousQuestions.reponse: b. A reduction in the usual price of something.

sousQuestions.texte: Receipt
sousQuestions.reponse: c. A written or printed document showing that you have paid for something.

sousQuestions.texte: Transaction
sousQuestions.reponse: e. An exchange or transfer of goods, services, or money.

sousQuestions.texte: Wholesaler
sousQuestions.reponse: f. A person or company that sells goods in large quantities to retailers.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences below with the correct words from the box to demonstrate your understanding of purchasing vocabulary.

Words: cash, credit card, mobile money, bank transfer, discount, receipt, price, expensive, cheap

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate word.
questions.reponse: 1. price, 2. cash, 3. credit card, 4. mobile money, 5. discount, 6. receipt, 7. expensive, 8. cheap, 9. bank transfer

sousQuestions.texte: The ______ of this phone is too high for me to afford.
He paid for the groceries in ______ because he didn't have his bank card.
She used her ______ to pay for the dress online.
______ is very popular in Cameroon for sending and receiving money.
The store offered a 20% ______ on all products during the sale.
Always ask for a ______ after making a payment.
This car is very ______; I can't afford it.
These shoes are ______, so many people can buy them.
For large amounts, a ______ is often safer than carrying cash.

enonce.texte: Identify whether each description refers to a customer, trader, wholesaler, retailer, producer, or consumer.

questions.reponse: 1. Wholesaler, 2. Retailer, 3. Producer, 4. Consumer, 5. Trader, 6. Customer

sousQuestions.texte: A person who buys goods in large quantities from the producer to sell to retailers.
A person or business that sells goods directly to the public in small quantities.
A person or company that makes or grows goods to sell.
A person who uses goods or services.
A person who buys and sells goods for a living.
A person who pays for goods or services in a shop.

enonce.texte:
Read the text about shopping methods and answer the questions that follow.

"In Cameroon, people have several options for purchasing goods and services. Traditional markets are popular for fresh produce and bargaining opportunities. Supermarkets offer convenience, fixed prices, and a wide range of products. Online shopping is becoming more common with platforms like Jumia and Afrimalin. Each method has its advantages and disadvantages. Traditional markets allow customers to negotiate prices but may lack product quality guarantees. Supermarkets provide a comfortable shopping experience but are often more expensive. Online shopping saves time and offers home delivery, but customers may face delivery delays or receive products that do not match the description. Payment methods also vary: cash is widely used in markets, while credit cards and mobile money are preferred for online transactions. The choice depends on individual preferences and needs."

questions.texte: List three shopping methods mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Traditional markets, supermarkets, and online shopping platforms.

questions.texte: What are the advantages of traditional markets according to the text?
questions.reponse: They offer fresh produce and allow customers to bargain or negotiate prices.

questions.texte: What are two disadvantages of online shopping?
questions.reponse: Delivery delays and receiving products that do not match the description.

questions.texte: What payment methods are preferred for online transactions?
Based on the text, why do people prefer supermarkets over traditional markets?
questions.reponse: Supermarkets offer convenience, fixed prices, and a wide range of products, even though they are often more expensive.

enonce.texte:
You and a friend are planning to buy new clothes for the upcoming school year. Write a dialogue between you (the customer) and a shop assistant. Your dialogue should be realistic and use vocabulary from the lesson.

Your dialogue should include:

  • A polite greeting
  • Asking about prices and discounts
  • Talking about payment methods
  • Asking for a receipt
  • A polite farewell

questions.texte: Write a complete dialogue between a customer and a shop assistant using appropriate vocabulary.
questions.reponse: Students should produce a realistic dialogue with proper structure, including greetings, questions about prices, negotiation, payment discussions, and farewells. The dialogue should use vocabulary such as price, discount, cash, credit card, receipt, transaction, customer, goods, etc.

enonce.texte:
You bought a mobile phone online. When it arrived, you discovered that it was damaged and did not work properly. Write a formal letter of complaint to the online store manager.

Your letter should include:

  • Your address and the date
  • The manager's address
  • A subject line
  • A clear description of the problem
  • The action you expect (refund, replacement, etc.)
  • A polite closing

questions.texte: Write a formal letter of complaint using appropriate vocabulary and tone.
questions.reponse: Students should write a well-structured formal letter with correct formatting (sender's address, recipient's address, date, subject line, salutation, body, closing). The letter should use vocabulary such as order, delivery, damaged, defective, refund, replacement, receipt, transaction, etc.

enonce.texte:
Study the table below comparing different shopping methods and answer the questions.

FeatureTraditional MarketSupermarketOnline Shopping
PriceNegotiableFixedUsually fixed
ConvenienceLimited hoursGoodVery good
Product rangeLimitedWideVery wide
Payment methodsMainly cashCash & cardCard, transfer, mobile money
Product inspectionYesYesNo

questions.texte: In which shopping method can prices be negotiated?
Which shopping method offers the widest range of products?
What is the main disadvantage of online shopping according to the table?
questions.reponse: Customers cannot inspect products before buying.

questions.texte: Which payment methods are available in supermarkets?
Compare the convenience of traditional markets and online shopping.
questions.reponse: Traditional markets have limited hours and require travel, while online shopping is available 24/7 and offers home delivery. Online shopping is therefore more convenient for most people.
